name: Poppyseed Strudel-Raisa Piotrowski's Recipe - Dkp
recipeIngredient: 2 packages Dry yeast or 1/4 lb Fresh yeast
recipeInstructions:
1 Dissolve the yeast and 1 tablespoon of sugar in the warm milk. Add 1 tablespoon of flour.
Let the mixture sit for about 10 minutes in a warm place (bubbles will indicate active yeast).
2 Gradually add all other ingredients to the yeast mixture and beat until soft dough forms.
If necessary, add more warm milk to achieve desired consistency.
3 Remove the dough from the bowl and knead on a floured board until blisters form on the surface.
4 Shape the dough into a ball, return it to the bowl, cover with wax paper, and let rise for 1 hour in a warm place (100°F).
5 Divide the dough into two balls. Roll each ball out to about 1/2 inch thickness.
6 Spread poppyseed filling evenly over one layer of dough, then roll up tightly from both ends.
7 Repeat with the second piece of dough and fillings.
8 Preheat oven to 325°F. Bake for 1 hour.
Note: I worked with Raisa when I was with CBS. She had come over from Russia and she and I would chat on the phone (she lived in New York). One day, I described this strudel recipe and asked her if she knew what it was and she sent me her recipe. Very good.
